opcode.name: OP_GuildList
opcode.comment: GuildListCode - S>C ~14KB at login, full server guild roster (list of guild-name strings). Confirmed 2026-05-27 (login-charmgmt): inline guild names in a 14176b login packet.

opcode.name: OP_MOTD
opcode.comment: MOTDCode - server message of the day, S>C world-stream post-EnterWorld. Confirmed 2026-05-27 (login-charmgmt): 154b payload = readable MOTD text ("Greetings Norrathians! ...").

opcode.name: OP_SendLoginInfo
opcode.comment: Client starting world session by asking for login information. Live: 464b C>S, first packet of every world session (5/5 confirmations).

opcode.name: OP_LogServer
opcode.comment: Live candidate: S>C 24b (0x7f8e), fires immediately after OP_SendLoginInfo in the handshake band.

opcode.name: OP_ApproveWorld
opcode.comment: Live candidate: small S>C in handshake band; not isolated yet.

opcode.name: OP_EnterWorld
opcode.comment: Client tells world server to enter world with selected char. Live: 72b C>S, char name (zero-padded) at offset 0 + 64b zeros + 0xffffffff trailer. 4/4 confirmations across char-create + login flows. Server reuses 0x0839 with shorter S>C variants (1b ack, 7b name echo) — XML maps the opcode to OP_EnterWorld for log naming.

opcode.name: OP_ExpansionInfo
opcode.comment: Which expansions user has. Live: 64b fixed S>C in the post-LogServer / pre-ApproveWorld handshake band (5/5 confirmations).

opcode.name: OP_SendCharInfo
opcode.comment: Send all chars visible @ char select. Live: 14176b S>C (variable with character roster size), fires once early in world handshake.

opcode.name: OP_SendSpellChecksum
opcode.comment: Contains a snippet of spell data. Live: 2056b C>S, payload tail shows caret-delimited spell-data text (matches spells_us.txt format). Capture-order position 2 of 4 in the post-handshake checksum burst.

opcode.name: OP_SendExeChecksum
opcode.comment: Client-side eqgame.exe checksum sample. Live: 2056b C>S, payload tail shows binary code-style content. Capture-order position 1 of 4 in the post-handshake checksum burst.

opcode.name: OP_SendBaseDataChecksum
opcode.comment: Client-side base-data file sample. Live: 2056b C>S, payload tail shows caret-delimited text records. Capture-order position 3 of 4 in the post-handshake checksum burst. Mapping to BaseData (vs another text-format file) inferred by elimination + position.

opcode.name: OP_SendSkillCapsChecksum
opcode.comment: Client-side skillcaps checksum. Live: 64b C>S, opaque hash-style payload. Capture-order position 4 of 4 in the post-handshake checksum burst — the only one not at 2056b, fits the smaller skillcaps data table.

opcode.name: OP_SetChatServer
opcode.comment: Legacy chatserver tuple. May be obsolete on Live — handshake capture shows only the SetChatServer2 catalog (no 56b S>C tuple observed, unlike Test).

opcode.name: OP_SetChatServer2
opcode.comment: Secondary chat / account / commerce service catalog. Live: 1464b S>C in handshake band (3/3 confirmations); ~18 plaintext Daybreak service URLs.

opcode.name: OP_ZoneServerInfo
opcode.comment: Zone server connection info. Live: 130b S>C, fires on world->zone handoff (zone server hostname + port). 2/2 confirmations.

opcode.name: OP_WorldComplete
opcode.comment: Client telling world server it is done. Live: 0b C>S, last C>S packet of session immediately after OP_ZoneServerInfo. World replies by disconnecting. 2/2 confirmations.

opcode.name: OP_DeleteCharacter
opcode.comment: Delete character @ char select. Live: 68b C>S world, name@offset 0. Re-confirmed post-2026-05-22 patch (login-charmgmt): 1 C>S fire carrying the deleted char name.

opcode.name: OP_CharacterCreate
opcode.comment: Create character @ char select. Live: 168b C>S world (name[64] + gender/race/class/deity/appearance fields), fires once per submitted creation right after OP_ApproveName response. Re-confirmed post-2026-05-22 patch (login-charmgmt).

opcode.name: OP_ApproveName
opcode.comment: Approving new character name @ char creation. Live: 84b C>S name candidate, 1b S>C status (taken vs available). Re-confirmed post-2026-05-22 patch (login-charmgmt): fires right before OP_CharacterCreate.

opcode.name: OP_RandomNameGenerator
opcode.comment: Returns a random name. May be obsolete on Live — repeated random-button presses during char creation produced no isolated wire request/response; names likely come pre-baked in char-select catalog dump.
